text.content:
Chapter 4 — A History of Ashes. The PCs ride into the Cinderlands to win the Shoanti's trust — and the lore of Kazavon. Begin at L10/11; reach 12th before the Deathhead Vault; 13th by chapter's end.

The history (GM): centuries ago the blue dragon Kazavon, champion of Zon-Kuthon, conquered Belkzen from Castle Scarwall — until Mandraivus of the Order of the Palatine Eye slew him with the bastard sword Serithtial. The dragon's remains would not be destroyed; seven survivors each carried one relic away in secret. The Shoanti shaman Amarund took the fangs — 'Midnight's Teeth' — to the Thassilonian pyramid on Conqueror's Bay… where Korvosa now stands. Her line guarded them for three hundred years. Ileosa found them anyway. And the Shoanti, watching their queen-enemy rise, now sharpen blades for a war that would only feed her.

NPC developments: Amin (if escorted out) runs with Cressida's rebels in the Gray District; Pilts (if alive) executes Cerulean Society envoys for sport — Boule may want him dead; Salvator (if rescued) leaves for Nidal with Laori's Brotherhood; if left behind, he dies trying to escape.

The PCs escort Vencarlo and Neolandus out of Korvosa to Harse. Cressida's parting kit: a wand of sending (10 charges), 2 scrolls of dream, and 4 scrolls of teleport for emergency returns (Bishop Keppira d'Bear of Pharasma relays sendings if no PC can).

Blackbird Ranch: five minutes north of Harse — Jasan Adriel's spread (ex-adventurer, 'the Blackbirds,' Vencarlo's old comrade). Trinia Sabor is here, leveled-up and delighted. After dinner, in the basement, Neolandus gives the full Kazavon Situation briefing (the Background above, as he knows it) and Vencarlo asks for the amulet of proof against detection from the Blackjack cache, if found, to hide Neolandus. Jasan outfits the party: trail rations, light warhorses, pack animals — free.

The Fourth Harrowing en route: past = Korvosa's founding on stolen ground; present = the PCs as fugitives mirroring the Shoanti exile; future = a city dangerous but topple-able. Foreshadow Cindermaw, the Moon temple, Bolt Rock, and the vault.

Factions in play all chapter: the Brotherhood of Bones (Shadowcount Sial + a chained kyton join @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori} in Kaer Maga; they scry the PCs and won't approach until Scarwall); the Red Mantis — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} runs the hunt from Korvosa via @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu's} sendings, hiring @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{the Cinderlander} as guide and buying the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwing gargoyles} with Mediogalti gold. Two scripted ambushes in Part 2; the reckoning in Part 3.

Respect Points (RP) — the chapter's spine, run as PF2e Victory Points. 20 RP → Thousand Bones declares the PCs worthy and the Sun Shaman speaks. −10 RP → branded enemies of the people; only battle remains. Show the running total; never reveal the target.

  • Shoanti PCs: +1 each (once); an exposed impostor (e.g. illusory disguise): −3.
  • Trophies: the severed head of a CR-9+ enemy of the Shoanti (great orcs, giants, dragons, the Cinderlander…), presented publicly to an elder within a week: +1 each, max 10 from trophies (@Check[type:society|dc:15] or any Shoanti to learn the custom).
  • Quests: per their entries (sredna +1–3; Akram +3; Cindermaw +2/+4; the Mark +3; the Trial +1/+1 per totem; Flameford +4; saving Krojun +3; Diplomacy at the Fire +2).
  • Losses: dead boneslayers −1d4 each; thundercallers routed and bad-mouthing −1d6; talking the moon maidens out of their fight −2d4; Krojun-rescues-the-party −1d6; refusing sredna −1; killing Cindermaw −1; public disrespect at GM discretion. The total can go negative.

Hundreds of skull-topped cairns (@Check[type:society|dc:15]: Skoan-Quah markers) — the three quahs' burial ground for 300 years. ~75 Skull Clan camp here under Chief One-Life, the shaman Ash Dancer, the wingless dragonne Wicked-Claws — and Thousand Bones, who remembers Gaekhen. A boneslayer patrol intercepts at half a mile; his name (or Gaekhen's) opens the camp: a guest yurt, water, and a visit to Gaekhen's cairn (he approves if told Rolth was punished).

Krojun asks Chief One-Life, 'Why do the Skoan-Quah harbor tshamek trespassers?'

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun Eats-What-He-Kills} arrives bearing the hero Berak's body and bristles at outsiders; Thousand Bones defuses with the funeral. Then the challenge: sredna (@Check[type:society|dc:20] recognizes the leather loop; @Check[type:society|dc:30] or ask, for his story). Refusing costs −1 RP. He is secretly testing them: he wants a reason not to march his people into Ileosa's jaws.

SREDNA (converted): two contestants on hands and knees, bound head-to-head by a loop. Rounds 1–3: opposed Intimidation — winner +2 points, tie +1 each (pulling early disqualifies). Round 4+: initiative; each turn Tug (opposed Athletics; the defender who Dug In last round gets +4 circumstance; win +2 points, lose/tie +2 to the foe) or Dig In (+4 vs. the next tug). After each round past the 4th: each contestant attempts a Fortitude save, DC = the opponent's current points — fail and collapse (both fail = draw; Endurance-style abilities grant +4). Krojun rages if it passes six rounds.

RP: force his rage +1 · lose gamely +1 · draw +2 · win +3. Story award for earning 2+ RP from the game, even in defeat.

pages.name: The Bone Council Fire & Skoan-Quah Support

text.content:
'You have already done my people a great favor by returning the body of one of our warriors. I sense now you come to me to ask something in return, yet know that by asking for this, you are helping us all. The Skoan-Quah are a peaceful people, yet we are also all but shunned by our kin. Our willingness to mix with tshamek shames many of my brothers and sisters in the other quahs. Only their respect for our tradition of guarding and protecting the dead of all Shoanti keeps them from open hostility against us.

My words do not reach their ears when I warn them of Queen Ileosa and her rise in power. They hear tales of the city in flames, of its king dead, of disease ravaging its people, and they see this as a just punishment for a hated enemy. My people do not see that a greater threat is growing in this turmoil. And now, you come to me with concerns, seeking the aid of my people. Speak of what you wish of the Shoanti, and perhaps we may find our needs are the same.'

Thousand Bones knows 'Kazavon' only as a name of fear: the lore lives with the Sklar-Quah Sun Shamans, and the Sun Clan kills tshamek on sight. The council names five paths to respect: become @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun's} nalharest · re-create Skurak's deed in @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw's} belly (Handout #4-1) · win Truthspeaker @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Akram's} endorsement · earn the Thrallkeeper's Mark at the Acropolis · survive the Sklar-Quah Trial of the Totem. (@Check[type:arcana|dc:30] or @Check[type:society|dc:30]: the 'Thrallkeeper's Mark' is the Sihedron rune of old Thassilon.)

Thousand Bones's gifts: 5 healing potions (moderate), a wand of create water (44 charges), a wand of endure elements (23), 5 pots of Shoanti war paint — and four @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.skoanBoneslayer1]{boneslayer} escorts: Ahalak, Hargev, Nalmid, Shadfrar (guides, witnesses, and the party's most fragile asset — see their entry for the RP stakes). In the wastes, @Check[type:perception|dc:40] confirms the distant riders shadowing the party are Krojun's — he evades all confrontation until the Acropolis.

pages.name: Quest A: The Acropolis of the Thrallkeepers

text.content:
The Acropolis — a Thassilonian conjuration complex whose masters died summoning the havero, 'Arms and Eyes of Forever,' from the Dark Tapestry; the part-summoned horror still hibernates below. Black granite flecked with blood-red crystal; every stone door bears a Sihedron bas-relief (press → it grinds up; descends after a minute; @Check[type:thievery|dc:15] jams one for a month; the closing crush deals @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] and pins — @Check[type:athletics|dc:25] to wrench free).

A crowded dungeon: 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} enter ~10 minutes behind the PCs (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{the Cinderlander} guided them but waits outside) — their real mission is to let @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu} watch the PCs fight through a scried assassin. And @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun} + 3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sklarThundercal1]{thundercallers} arrived an hour before, hiding at the observation gallery (A3) to take the party's measure (he mocks the Mark-quest as 'stargazing' — his laugh adds 2 Noise Points). @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:31] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:23] brings the Shoanti in against the Mantis early (intimidated, they leave after and bad-mouth). The Shoanti automatically aid against the havero. Save them from certain death and Krojun may declare nalharest on the spot.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
The Mantis tailModerate · 80 XP @ L11

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} — they strike once discovered (or as the PCs leave, if never engaged). Koriantu is watching; let the players feel it.

pages.name: A1-A3. Thrallkeeper's Walk, the Pool & the Gallery

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A1-A3 — p.209.

The air in this massive, cathedral-like space seems strangely cool. The walls are carved with vertical ridges that rise to support the arch above, where the ceiling vaults into the shadows to a height of nearly sixty feet. A five-foot-wide balcony rings the room, the floor of which drops fifteen feet into a pool of dark water. Halfway between the east and west ends of the room, a bridge crosses the pool. Two large stone doors stand in the walls to the south and east. Smaller doors sit in the walls to the northwest and southwest-all four doors bear depictions of seven-pointed-stars.

A1's dust shows tracks (@Check[type:survival|dc:19] follows them to the A2/A3 door). A2, Pool of the Havero: a 60-ft vault over a 15-ft pool floored with the sleeping horror's rubbery flank — the bottom is dimensionally adjacent to a thing orbiting a dead star.

NOISE POINTS (track openly): verbal spell +1 · running +1/runner · yell +1 · combat +5/round · sonic effect +10 · damaging the flank +1/point. Noise in A2 doubles. The total decays 1d10/minute. Thresholds (trigger the round after): 10 Twitch · 20 Investigate (1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{tentacle}) · ~30 Seek · 40 Assault (2) · 50 Wrath (4, the max). Each slain tentacle −1d20 noise; below 10, all retract (quelled 1 hour). 20+ tentacles slain in one fight also quells it. Story award for crossing without waking it — or for quelling it once woken.

  • ≤4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{Havero Tentacle (9)}
Tentacle Wrath (worst case)Moderate · 80 XP @ L11

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{havero tentacles} at 60-ft reach, attackable anywhere along their lengths. The Mantis watch; the Shoanti help.

pages.name: A4-A7. Shrines, the Illumacore & the Eternal Glyphs

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A4-A7 — p.211.

A4: a six-winged serpent-woman shrine (@Check[type:religion|dc:25]: Kurshu the Undying, Lissala's herald) — sapphire and emerald slivers in the tail, ~320 gp re-scaled (the thundercallers hoot at it: +2 Noise). A5, the Illumacore: a rune-ringed shaft of emerald light (@Check[type:arcana|dc:26] to deduce): step in → feather fall 70 ft down to A9; cross the lower ring → levitate back up. Krojun goes first, to show off. A6: a bronze Lissala statue — her jade whip is a +2 axiomatic whip, cursed (1 Wisdom drain/day of visions; −6 Will saves while Thassilonian writing is visible; lifts only re-handed to the statue or remove curse vs. 18th). The base-drawer frame (@Check[type:thievery|dc:20]) projects the original acropolis map on the ceiling via the illumacore.

A7: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.eternalGlyphs001]{Eternal Glyphs} — and the gear of the Shoanti dead who starved reading (+1 hide, a +1 striking frost earth breaker, a +1 striking returning totem spear). If Krojun must drag the party out: −1d6 RP.

pages.name: A8-A11. The Lower Level & the Golarion Globe

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A8-A11 — p.213.

A jumble of five crushed humanoid skeletons lie in the southeast corner of this chamber. Stone niches in the southern wall contain a few pieces of clutter.

A9: beyond the havero's reach (the Mantis love an ambush at the illumacore landing). A 120-ton stone plug (@Check[type:perception|dc:22]) hides A10, the Fallback Vault (gaseous-form gap): the five dead Thrallkeepers' gear — a bottle of air, decanter of endless water, sustaining spoon, scrolls (2× clairvoyance, 2× sending, 2× dimension door), a +1 striking magical-beast-bane dagger, a bright-orange cloak of resistance +3 (greater resilient rune), a scabbard of keen edges, and ~700 gp re-scaled in jewelry and a Lissalan holy symbol.

A11, the Golarion Globe: a hollow 10-ft stone globe of the pre-Earthfall world (@Check[type:society|dc:20]) floating on three immovable rods. Touch it and contemplate the stars → drawn inside into suspended animation and space-visions (@Check[type:will|dc:15] per minute or 1d4 Intelligence drain; concentrate on Golarion to exit; auto-ejected at Int 0). Exit grants the THRALLKEEPER'S MARK (one palm, one week, once per year): right palm = banishment 1/day; left = summon (5th rank) 1/day; either grants +4 status vs. mental. Pulling a rod drops the globe (+10 Noise; it cracks — 1d4 more weeks of function). +3 RP for gaining the Mark before Shoanti eyes; story award if any PC gains it.

pages.name: Quest B: The House of the Moon

text.content:
A silvery stone shrine stands atop a low promontory in the foothills here. The surrounding stone has been smoothed by ages of wind, but the shrine itself remains as stark and unweathered as the day its final block was set in place. The structure shimmers with a slightly reflective sheen, and even on moonless nights it glimmers with lunar radiance. A 30-foot-tall opening allows access to the shrine interior at ground level.

The curving sides of the archway are framed by the long peacock-like tail feathers of an immense star monarch carved into the building's facade. The creature's wings furl to the left and right, fanning over two side towers attached to the central spire, which rises to a height of 100 feet. Above, a silvery sphere caps the central tower's peak, as if the moon had fallen out of the sky to become impaled upon the steeple.

Thousand Bones's pointer: Truthspeaker Akram arrives with the Lyrune-Quah in days — but a red reaver has taken the shrine, eating a dozen moon maidens and their commander. Tekrakai's surviving band of four (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:30] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:23] to bench them — but doing so costs −2d4 RP) wants it dead; her briefing is exact: rending claws, a discord-roar, distracted by artistic beauty, never sleeps.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redReaver0000001]{Red Reaver (13)}
  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.moonMaiden000001]{Moon Maiden (6, allies)}
The Red ReaverModerate · 80 XP @ L11

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redReaver0000001]{the Red Reaver} (at ~85% HP from old wounds) in the moonlit hall — walls of self-repairing blessed masonry (Hardness 16; SR vs. manipulation). It targets the heaviest armor, pursues half a mile, fights to the death. A performance (@Check[type:performance|dc:31]) buys calm rounds. Story award per surviving maiden.

Among the seven dead maidens: 3× +1 hide armor and 5× +1 starknives (the Lyrune-Quah gift them to helpers). The shrine itself: Desnan sleepers receive a yearly dream-commune from the avoral Nightspear; the sky-well projects the true night sky at all hours.

After: the tribe arrives; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Akram} agrees to travel as witness (+3 RP, story award; second award if he lives to the Sun Shaman's tale). He is 68 and frail; honor forbids him an escort. Keep. Him. Alive.

pages.name: Quest C: The Belly of the Beast (Cindermaw)

text.content:
Skurak was a great warrior and greater traitor to the Sklar-Quah. He slew his brother, a man of even greater courage. To the Sklar-Quah, family is purity-crimes against family are the greatest one can commit. Although Skurak claimed the death was an accident that occurred while he and his brother were hunting, others spoke of murder spawned of jealous rage. Skurak was declared a tshamek by the Sun Shaman and cast out. But before Skurak left, he said he would be born again and return to his tribe.

The deed: one PC swallowed by @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw} and out again, before Shoanti witnesses. The method is irrelevant — cut free (Rupture 30; fire resistance advised), teleport from the belly (concentration inside), even charm. Tempting a swallow: Deception at −2 vs. its Sense Motive. An illusion of the deed must beat the witnesses' saves and radiate heat. Hunt it near the western cindercones; it finds loiterers — stage its spark-gout surfacings first.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw (14)}
Cindermaw (do not slay)Severe · 120 XP @ L11

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw} — Severe+ if fought honestly, which is the wrong lesson: killing it costs RP (a dead god 'wasn't that tough'). +2 RP for the deed; +4 if the worm lives.

pages.name: Quest D: Flameford & the Trial of the Totem

text.content:
Flameford — 52 Sklar-Quah on a cliff-edge behind permanent spike stones fields (@Check[type:perception|dc:29] per square; hidden zigzag paths), under Chief Ready-Klar (Krojun's uncle) and the nameless Sun Shaman. A six-rider burn-rider patrol laughs at the PCs' goal but escorts them in, armed.

At the Foot of the Fire: the whole camp gathers; @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:29] mollifies the chieftain (+10 circumstance with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Akram}; @Check[type:intimidation|dc:24] works instead) — +2 RP. At 20 RP, skip to the Assault; under it, the Sun Shaman grants the Trial of the Totem (Krojun storms off on a long ride). Mentioning Kazavon makes the old man pale and refuse. Akram departs before the Sklar-Quah dawn, advising: win Krojun.

THE TRIAL: haul fired-clay sun totems (10 ft, 500 lbs; Hardness 6, HP 40) up Bolt Rock — to the 30-ft tier by day one, the 40-ft mesa by day two, each kept standing through its day; the third dawn crowns success. Teamwork legal; no food or water provided (bring your own; endure elements and create food are fair); altering totem or rock is cheating — the Sun Shaman inspects (@Check[type:survival|dc:20] per alteration to hide; 3+ immovable rods leave no trace). Steadying: @Check[type:athletics|dc:12] every 6 hours; a failure leans the totem (@Check[type:athletics|dc:16] to catch); a second topples it (@Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] to the totem — shattered = that PC is out). Day two without sleep: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}; run heat and thirst by the environment rules.

The Sun Vision: each hour fatigued, 10% cumulative chance (+30%/hour exhausted) of the aurochs-vision — two easy Wisdom-flavored insights: she is the aurochs… and she is not prey. A PC who completes it gains +1 insight to attack and damage vs. the Red Mantis and their minions for the chapter. The message: stop waiting. Turn and face them.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.CzxQpB3p0d9hwPeR]{Bulette (8)}
Hungry Visitors (day 2, exhausted)Moderate · 90 XP @ L11

6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.CzxQpB3p0d9hwPeR]{bulettes} leap the lower tier (2d4 rounds of burrowing warning). Fighting one-handed on a totem: -2 to attacks; a bulette passing a totem forces @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] or it topples; misses by 3 or less against a holder strike the totem half the time. Dropping a totem on a bulette: @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] (@Check[type:reflex|dc:15] avoids) — and the totem takes the same.

The Third Dawn: the tribe ascends pre-dawn; one standing totem makes the whole party Sklar-Quah-accepted (its keeper a citizen; the rest honored nalharests). The Sun Shaman casts lesser restoration on each PC. +1 RP for the attempt, +1 per totem that never toppled; story award if at least one stood. Krojun, returned and calmed, watches day two from the rocks and grudgingly calls the PCs lucky in their nalharests.

The Assault triggers at 20 RP: the Sun Shaman must first commune at the Kallow Mounds (wind walk, back in a day; Krojun commands). At dusk the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwings} lift the Red Mantis over the spike-fields and drop them among the yurts.

  • 3-6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
  • 3-6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwing Gargoyle (6)}
The AssaultLow · 60 XP @ L11

If the vault still stands / PCs L11: 3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} + 3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwing gargoyles}. If the hideout fell first / PCs L12: double it (6+6 — Moderate · 90 XP @ L12), adding @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} if she escaped with a grudge. The lead assassin names the PCs and offers the Shoanti peace for their heads: with 5+ RP the Shoanti refuse; with 4 or fewer they stand aside and the PCs face the full force (6 assassins, the Cinderlander, two dozen gargoyles). The tribal battle runs offstage: if the PCs win their fight, most Shoanti live. +4 RP for breaking the assault.

Saving Krojun: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{Cinderlander's} price for guiding the Mantis was Krojun. As the PCs finish their fight, the duel still rages — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun} at ~30 HP against the hunter's ~100 (Low · 60 XP @ L11). Unaided, Krojun dies and the killer walks. Intervene: the Cinderlander flees below ~20 HP; Krojun, saved, declares the PCs nalharest — +3 RP and a story award equal to his own defeat. The aftermath is a victory feast: sredna rematches, Sklar-Quah tattoos, a marriage offer contingent on shaving a PC's head.

If the PCs fall, the Shoanti are wiped out — the merciful out: survivors wake stripped and imprisoned in the Deathhead Vault, which conveniently is where Part 3 goes anyway.

Part 3 — Korvosa, meanwhile: the Sable Company disbanded, the Hellknights withdrawn, the Guard gutted; Abadar's archbanker and the Asmodeans back the queen (seeding schism among young clergy); only Pharasma's church quietly resists; the Red Mantis kill notables in daylight; Ileosa's endgame — thousands sacrificed for eternal youth — gathers. Do not start before L12. Keppira d'Bear's sending recalls the PCs: 'Return to Korvosa! An opportunity to strike at the heart of the queen's power…'

An Unexpected Opportunity: the rebel HQ is the old Dead Warrens ossuary (Ch.1!), warded and password-keyed ('Blackjack'). Guildmaster Boule of the Cerulean Society (@Check[type:society|dc:20]) wants the D16 Arkona papers — offering 600 pp per PC (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:25] raises it to 900; story award for brokering favors instead of coin, Kroft's preference). His intel: the Society's old derro-tunnel back door into the Deathhead Vault beneath the Longacre Building — now Red Mantis + Gray Maiden territory. His extras: a partial key, directions (+20 circumstance to the E1/E2, E6, and E9 secret doors) — and fake 'Revolution ciphers' leaked to @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{Kordaitra}, nailing the would-be codebreaker to her quarters for a week.

Objectives (Kroft): rescue Marcus Endrin · recover the records · learn why Arbiter Zenobia sided with the queen · break the Red Mantis and Gray Maiden leadership. Doors: stone in the west (E1-E9: Hardness 8, HP 60), locked reinforced wood in the Vault proper (E10-E21: @Check[type:thievery|dc:40] or carried keys).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E1-E4 — p.237.

A ten-foot-wide trough of murky water fills two-thirds of this wide, brick-lined tunnel. A slimy five-foot-wide walkway runs along the east side of the waterway. A wall of rusty iron bars with an equally rusty gate atop the walkway blocks passage further into the tunnel. Further to the south, the tunnel deadends at an ancient collapse.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.RA2dkQJANNFjgdSt]{Catoblepas (12)}
E1 — SnagglebreathTrivial · 40 XP @ L12

An albino @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.RA2dkQJANNFjgdSt]{catoblepas} — Cinnabar's imported pet (@Check[type:perception|dc:15] spots the painted rust; its mithral-spiked collar, engraved 'Snagglebreath,' ~100 gp re-scaled). It loves Red Mantis colors and savages everyone else. The secret door beyond: @Check[type:perception|dc:40] (+20 with Boule's directions).

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.DlRe4c2XlBSpwmms]{Mogmora (7, Elite)}
E2 — the 'Sewer Brewer'Trivial · 0 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.DlRe4c2XlBSpwmms]{Mogmora} (an Elite greater barghest in goblin form), planar-bound bartender of a dressed-up tavern, offering free ale poisoned with lich dust (party-size + 4 doses behind the bar). He stalls 1d4 rounds cataloguing the party, then feigns terror and dimension doors to E6 to report.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.dispellingMist01]{Dispelling Mist (8)}
  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.manananggal00001]{Manananggal (9)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
E3 — the ChokepointTrivial · 45 XP @ L12

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.dispellingMist01]{Dispelling Mist} at the entry; a 60-ft pit bridged by a permanent-image plank (@Check[type:will|dc:17] on interaction); cytillesh patches (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied} creep within 20 ft per day); and 2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.manananggal00001]{manananggals} — the late Sable officers Banzul and Wevenner — plus a hidden @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassin} who watches two rounds, then slips to E4 to report. Their torsos lie in E8: destroy those to end them at dawn.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cytillipede00001]{Cytillipede (8)}
E4 — the Temple of AchaekekTrivial · 40 XP @ L12

A crescent cathedral-cavern (the mantis façade is fresh stone shape, @Check[type:arcana|dc:28]; @Check[type:religion|dc:15] names Achaekek). 2 resting @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{assassins} + the mercy-bought @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cytillipede00001]{cytillipede}, whose flash is timed for maximum coverage. If the alarm is up, everything that fled converges here, with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu} behind (Extreme · 175 XP @ L12 — the vault's worst case).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E5-E6 — p.242.

Three large stone vats sit in alcoves in this room. Each is big enough to hold a human, but apart from a strange blue residue caking the insides of each, they are empty. A cot and a writing desk sit amid the vats-awkward additions of comfort in what once seems to have been a strange laboratory.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar (13)}
E5 — CinnabarLow · 60 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} in her spartan chambers — masked, mantis-formed, summoning. Read her morale before running this: the kneeling surrender at ~10 HP is a genuine fork in the campaign. @Check[type:perception|dc:25] at the desk finds a crimson parchment under secret page — speak 'Kayltanya' to reveal Handout #4-2, the Mistress's letter binding the Mantis contract to the Crown.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu (13)}
E6 — the Inner SanctumLow · 60 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu} behind her screen-illusion of slaughter-murals and a towering mantis idol (@Check[type:will|dc:32] on interaction; only the pillars are real). Blade barrier, void, and zero personal stake in the PCs — she came to document Cinnabar's failure. South secret door: @Check[type:perception|dc:40] (+20 from Boule).

pages.name: E7-E11. Crypts, the Daemon & the Mother of Thorns

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E7-E11 — p.245.

The walls of this large room bear ancient carvings of wild animals in a scene set within a rugged, barren landscape. Two sarcophagi, their lids pushed aside, sit in alcoves to the north and east, while to the west is a third sarcophagus upon a raised dais surrounded by several three-foot-tall vats. The rank smell of vinegar fills the air.

E7: looted Shoanti sarcophagi (@Check[type:society|dc:25]), once derro cages. E8, Zenobia's Crypt: by night her headless body lies in the west sarcophagus and the manananggals' torsos in the others (@Check[type:religion|dc:27]: destroying torsos destroys their owners; destroying Zenobia's body only ends her living masquerade).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vylothMeladaem01]{Vyloth (13)}
E8 — VylothLow · 60 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vylothMeladaem01]{Vyloth}, the bored famine-daemon, opens with his lying question-game — then Horrid Wilting. If the body is destroyed he teleports to Zenobia (E18) and is fought there instead.

E9: the Society's secret stair (@Check[type:perception|dc:40], +20 from Boule). E10: the Final Walk — the condemned's glowing ivory corridor, its woven calm emotions long disarmed.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.motherOfThorns01]{Mother of Thorns (13)}
E11 — the Mother of ThornsLow · 60 XP @ L12

The old execution chamber, now the scar-carving room: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.motherOfThorns01]{the Mother of Thorns} attacks on sight — vines at 15 feet, fear to scatter, domination for the strongest — narrating to Kordaitra telepathically all the while.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E12-E21 — p.248.

This grim chamber is outfitted with all manner of torture implements: a rack, several cages, a cramped stockade with spiky bars, and smaller tools of torture like thumbscrews and iron boots sitting on shelves. A caged-off area to the west serves as a guard post.

  • E12 — Observation office: secret doors @Check[type:perception|dc:30].
  • E13 — Cells: 12 imprisoned recruits who refused the Maidens (Boule's or Kordaitra's keys, or @Check[type:thievery|dc:40]) — story award per woman freed (15 total with E18).
  • E14 — The secret cell: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.marcusEndrin0001]{Marcus Endrin} (see his entry; the E20 records reveal the door).
  • E15/E16 — Guard posts: 4 + 4 resting @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenGuard1]{Gray Maiden Guards} (~5 minutes to armor up).
  • E17 — Tisharue: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.tisharueWarden01]{the warden}, her heirloom +2 resilient mithral chain, and her dossier: Sabina Merrin was barely conditioned.
  • E19 — Vavana's room: empty (reassigned to the castle — a Ch.6 thread).
  • E20 — The records: every woman taken, every 'traitor' executed, Endrin's cell — story award.
  • E21 — Kordaitra: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{the conditioner} at her ciphers; the 'Sinister Sister' painting (~450 gp); the cache: a bag of holding IV of potions, dust of disappearance, a wand of cure critical wounds, scrolls of break enchantment, heal, limited wish, resurrection.
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zenobiaZenderh01]{Zenobia (11)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{Kordaitra (10)}
  • 5× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenGuard1]{Gray Maiden Guard (6)}
E18 — the Maiden's Nursery (climax)Trivial · 30 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zenobiaZenderh01]{Zenobia} mid-indoctrination behind a 5-@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenGuard1]{guard} watch post; on the alarm @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{Kordaitra} relocates here (Trivial · 50 XP @ L12), and the Mother of Thorns or Vyloth may fall back per their morale. The vault's true boss-pile — thin it before it stacks.

Bolstering the Rebellion: the vault broken, the Red Mantis quit Korvosa (returning only with Mistress Kayltanya in Ch.6), the Maidens fall back to the castle, martial law ends — run Korvosa as Unrest hereafter, and let the Epidemic Clock ease accordingly.

'Many hundreds of years ago, a man named Mandraivus gathered a small group of heroes to fight a despotic blue dragon named Kazavon, and my ancestor was one who joined this crusade. This ancestor, a shaman named Amarund, was gone for months. When she finally returned she was not the same woman-her hands shook, her eyes carried a haunted stare, and she cried out in fear at night. She spoke little of what she had experienced while fighting at Mandraivus's side, but did say that they were successful in defeating Kazavon and that the dragon's fortress of Scarwall in Belkzen was now under Mandraivus's control.

The Sun Shaman's Tale (at 20 RP): Mandraivus's seven survivors; Amarund's fangs in the pyramid beneath what is now Korvosa; the seeds of Kazavon's soul — Ileosa carries two souls, and while the second ripens she commands her own mortality (he nods grimly at news of the failed assassination). The counterstroke sleeps at Scarwall in Belkzen, where Serithtial waits among the chained dead. Chapter 5's road.

The Blessing of the Ancestors (Bolt Rock, two hours before dawn; the conduit is Zellara — her deck-bearer feels the empathic wave): the spirit greets the PCs by name, offers the fifth Harrowing, and sings the Spirit's Song (Handout #4-3). Boons: each PC infuses one owned weapon (undead-bane) or armor/shield (ghost touch) — functioning only within Castle Scarwall — and gains Infused Soul: the next failed save vs. a death effect is negated instead (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} for a round; once, works anywhere). Ready-Klar proclaims the PCs Friends of the Sun.
